TRANSCRIPT / Bonus / Book Launch Prelaunch, Part 2
TRANSCRIPT
Okay walking forward, you know I started off the Pre-Prelaunch with the survey for my
99 designs book cover contest and that was the first word that anyone knew that
anything, that I had a book coming out.
Then this was May 4th, I released this video here and what I did was I wrote an
email…sent an email out to my list. The email subject line was "the book is finally
done" and then in parentheses "behind the scenes worries." I sent this out and it
basically says, "Finally, I just sent my book manuscript off to the publisher, it's literally
been years in the making and now it's done. I'm excited, I'm celebrating and I want to
share some of the lessons learned and the mistakes I almost made.” Then I gave a link
here - "lessons from finishing my book. PS, I had a crazy idea right before I shot the
video, not sure if it's a great idea or not but I left it in the video. Let me know what you
think.”
This is a very short, simple email. A couple of things, first of all I was going after a little
curiosity here, the mistake I almost made, I had a crazy idea but I left it in the video.
Those are curiosity hooks, you know, right above the links trying to get them to click
on the link.
Up here, I talk about the manuscript taking forever and this was something…you
know one of the things I wanted to do was, a lot of people, there are some people in
my market that'll sit there and take something and write a report up, a three page
report in a couple hours or 30 minutes and release it on Kindle and call it a Kindle
book. I wanted people to make sure that this was a real book, that it's something that I
had written and that I had written, that I had taken a lot of care with.
They click on that link, it took them over to my blog here. The title of the blog post,
"My Book is Finally Done (Behind the Scenes)." This copy here was pretty much, this
copy up here is the same copy that's in the email. Then I talk about a launch party,
watch the video to learn more. Just trying to get them to watch the video. And then…
Jeff - Video: 	“Hey, it's Jeff Walker and I am really excited today because I just
finished my manuscript for my book here. Here it is, just came back
from the proofreaders. It's got red ink all the way through it. We're
done with this in a couple hours. I'm just going to basically zip this up
and send it off to our book designer. She's going to send it over to
our publisher on Monday. Very excited. The book is, here's the title.
It's a long one. "Launch, an Internet Millionaire's Secret Formula to

Sell Almost Anything Online, Build a Business You Love and Live the
Life of Your Dreams." Long subtitle, but there's a lot to cover there
because of course it, you know, this book it's my life, it's what we've
done with Product Launch Formula. It's my life and all my product
launch formula owner's lives. It's been such a huge thing in the world

Released this… this is basically letting people know that something is coming. I'm
showing the actual, physical manuscript so they again, getting that this is a real
substantive book that I put a lot of effort into…that we bothered to get proofreading. I
know it sounds crazy but in my market, sometimes people release some crazy things.
Real strong reaction. Big social reaction. It got 203 shares, 192 comments, so this is a
really successful video. Again, it's just about letting people know something is coming
and showing them how them excited I am about it. That was on May 4th. Now
remember the publication date was the last week of June. We are about six, seven,
eight weeks out from the actual launch.
Moving forward, this is two weeks later, so May 18th. Exactly two weeks later, I
released another video. This is "The Real Reason I Wrote a Book (+ Book Update)."
Again I sent out a very simple email, drove people into this blog post. Talked about
the reaction to the prior blog post, it was amazing. Over 170 comments, so there's
social proof. I'm showing that social proof. Then I take them down and show them the
video.
Jeff - Video : 	“Hey, it's Jeff Walker and a couple weeks ago, I published a blog post
about my upcoming book. That was really the first time I really put it
out in public that I was going to release this book. You know I've
been working on it for years, literally, but I hadn't really mentioned it
in public, but I did. Got a big response, had something like 160, 170
comments, so that was really cool. People seem pretty excited. This is
like one of those deals, you just keeping on making these baby-steps
and here we go. Pretty exciting. I'm excited. I'll tell you what, this
video is about me practicing holding my book. I think this is sort of
the way you do it. You know, supposed to hold it so you can see the
cover and everything like that. I'm going to be shooting some videos
for ..remember I'm going to have a Prelaunch for this book. It's going
to be cool and things are going to be starting pretty soon.
Publication date is June 24th, so like it's what, we're about five weeks
out or something like this.”

TRANSCRIPT / Bonus / Book Launch Prelaunch, Part 2
So there you have it, I released the date now at this point. A little bit of trivia, that book
I'm holding is not actually the real book. I didn't have the book back from the
publisher yet, but I wanted to show people the cover. I wanted to have a reason to
shoot a video. I wanted, again, to understand that it was real, so I printed out a copy,
you know because I had the graphic of the cover. I printed that out on my inkjet
printer and just cut out the paper and I taped it on top of another book. It's a
simulated book right there. I did release the date. Again, this is on May 18th, so we're
about five weeks out from the publication.
This is the third piece of Pre-Prelaunch where the first one was that the survey for the
book cover design. The second one was showing them the manuscript here. The third
one, I'm showing them the actual book. As you get deeper into this video, I start to
talk about what's in the book. Again, just trying to get people activated and get
excited. Again this one had a strong reaction as well. Let's see, what did we get? Got
about 266 comments. Very strong reaction to this one as well.
Then next we have, this is the unboxing. The official unboxing. This is when I actually
got the books and this was pretty fun because this was all…I’ll show you how it plays
out here, but really this is 100% legit. We were having a team meeting and literally in
the middle of that team meeting at a hotel here in Durango, the books got delivered.
This is what it looked like.
Daniel - Video: 	“What am I shooting?”
Jeff - Video : 	“This is the unveiling of the book.”
Daniel - Video: 	“And we're shooting it on an iPhone.”
Jeff - Video : 	“This is the first I've seen it…looks better than my homemade
version. It really does look a lot better.”
Speaker 6 video: 	“That's awesome.”
Speaker 4 video: 	“First out of the box, wow.”
Jeff - Video : 	“Yes. Feels good.”
Speaker 5 video: 	“You're not going to sign these?”
Speaker 6 video: 	“Wow, kind of looks like it's waterproof.”
Speaker 5 video: 	“We should line up for book signing.”

In this video, basically it was a fun moment. It was a spontaneous moment and so we
had this video and just if you even hear at the beginning…you know, my son is my
videographer and he, of course, wants to shoot everything with fancy cameras and
everything. I said, "I want to get at that box, start shooting this." He's like, "With an
iPhone?" I'm like "Yes!" He shot that and then what happened then after is then I
came back to put a wrap on it.
Jeff - Video : 	“My unboxing of my book launch and actually we've got a team off-
site and the books just happened to get shipped in right in time. I
opened it up and was passing it out to my team members. It was
awesome, it was fun. A little…well, it was intense. We were basically
planning out…”
Again, giving people the behind-the-scenes with the book and here…so this is
actually June 16th. This is only about a week out from the actual publication date. By
this time, we were in the pre-order period where people could pre-order and you'll
see I actually underneath the video, I had a link for them to pre-order. In the video I
also gave them the link and told them to go pre-order. Here we've moved out of just
a pure Pre-Pre getting people excited and now we actually have a call to action and
we're driving people to actually go ahead and pre-order the book.
I just want to show you a couple other videos here that are beyond Prelaunch but this
is where I announced that "Launch" is a number one New York Times bestseller, which
was pretty awesome. It was a very exciting moment when I learned that. We shot this
video just shortly after I learned it so this is just me being really excited. You know, this
is just the idea of finding a way to keep the book in front of my tribe’s…you know,
front and center for my tribe. Keeping it in front of them. For anyone who hasn't read
about it or hasn't told their friends about it, I just…you know people love a winner
and I had a winner. It was a number one New York Times bestseller. I wanted to keep
that front and center for people.

because it just got announced a couple of hours ago as I shoot this.
On the New York Times site. And I'm so excited, when I got the news I
was like literally shaking. I was like bouncing off the walls.”
This is just a…let’s see, we had 273 comments on this one. This one got a really strong
reaction and it's letting people know that we've got a winner. People love jumping
onboard with a winner. That was July 13th. Then just a week later, came out with this
one, which talks a little bit about how I put it on the best seller list. Again, people love
behind the scenes and here we go.
Jeff - Video : 	“Hey, it's Jeff Walker, I'm the author of the number one bestselling
New York Times bestselling book, "Launch." Very excited still about
that. The number this week is two because this is our second week on
the list. We did slip from number one to number two. I'm still pretty
happy about that. That "Five Love Languages," I think, beat us out,
that's been a juggernaut and it's been on the list for over a year now.
That beat us out of number one, so we're sitting at number two in our
second week…”
Again, this is just extending the momentum. That's what this is about is you know, we
had some good news and we want to keep it rolling. There you go, that was the Pre-
Prelaunch, the Prelaunch and I gave you a little bit of the extending of the momentum
